text: [Music]
Hi, Bob the Canadian here. There will be
times when you're having an English
conversation where someone will ask you
a question and you will not have the
answer. And in that situation, you will
need to say, "I do not know." They might
ask a question like, "Why is the sky
blue?" I do not know. Why did your cat
run away? I do not know. Why did you eat
the last cookie? I do not know. Now,
that is the slow, clear, and well
pronounced version of that answer. There
are two faster versions, and the first
is proper English, and it's contracting
the words do and not into don't. So,
you'll have an answer like I don't know.
Why did you get a speeding ticket? I
don't know. Why did you eat the last
cookie? I don't know. And the next
version, which is even faster, is not
really proper written English, but you
will hear it quite often in English
conversation, and it looks like this and
sounds like this. I don't know. I don't
know. Why did you lose your wallet? I
don't know. Why did you eat the last
cookie? I don't know. Why didn't you buy
tickets to the baseball game? I don't
know. Why did you get a speeding ticket?
I don't know.
Well, that's the response. I do not
know. I don't know. I don't know. Bob
the Canadian here. Thanks for watching.
Don't forget to subscribe and have a
great day. I don't know. I don't know. I
don't know. Why'd you run so fast? I
don't know. Why'd you eat that stick? I
don't know. Why'd you let the dog out? I
don't know.
Why'd you eat all the cookies? I don't
know. Why'd you get a speeding ticket? I
don't know. Why' you jump out of that
tree? I don't know. Why'd you
steal my candies? I don't know. Why' you
Why didn't you do your homework? I don't
know.
[Music]
